oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Connecting pastures: Navigating social media for veterinarians
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Schack, Michelle
oai_dc:dc.dc:subject: social media
veterinarians
communication
agriculture
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: Veterinarians hold a unique position of trust with both produc­ers and consumers, serving as credible voices on animal health and welfare. Yet, consumers remain increasingly disconnected from modern agriculture, creating gaps in understanding and trust. Social media provides veterinarians with a powerful platform to share expertise, foster transparency, and engage directly with diverse audiences. This paper outlines the oppor­tunities and challenges for veterinarians using social media, including establishing credibility, handling misinformation, building a professional brand, and addressing negativity on­line. Examples from past agricultural practices, such as tail docking and downer cow regulations, highlight the importance of accurate communication and adapting to evolving public ex­pectations. With practical strategies to get started, veterinarians can leverage social media to educate, enhance practice visibility, and strengthen connections between agriculture and society.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; Vol. 58 No. 2 (2025): 2025 AABP Annual Conference Proceedings; 184-185
oai_dc:dc.dc:rights: Copyright (c) 2026 American Association of Bovine Practitioners
